sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Atalanta have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins and suffering 2 losses, with a strong attacking presence led by Gianluca Scamacca (4 goals recently). Roma, on the other hand, have been more consistent, winning 3 of their last 5, including a convincing 3-0 away win against Celtic, with Evan Ferguson in fine form (3 goals).

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Atalanta have dominated Roma, winning both matches (2-1 at home and 2-0 away). The Bergamaschi have a strong historical edge, with 4 wins and 1 draw in their last 5 head-to-head clashes, often scoring first and maintaining an unbeaten streak in 6 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Roma sit higher in the league table (4th, 33 points) compared to Atalanta (10th, 22 points), showcasing a more solid defense (11 goals conceded vs. Atalanta’s 19). Atalanta, however, excel in possession (57.1%, 5th in the league) and final third entries (29.4 per game), which could challenge Roma’s backline.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Atalanta will miss key forward Ademola Lookman, potentially impacting their attack, but Scamacca and Charles De Ketelaere remain threats. Roma are without midfielder Lorenzo Pellegrini, which might affect their creativity, though Matías Soulé (5 goals, 3 assists) could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Atalanta’s home advantage and historical dominance over Roma, combined with their attacking metrics, they might edge this contest despite Roma’s superior league form. Sofascore’s close prediction (35% home win, 31% draw, 34% away win) suggests a tight game, likely with both teams scoring as seen in 4 of their last 5 meetings.
